---
title: Kitchen Sink
subtitle: Every Feature in One File
author: Test Author
date: 2026-03-25
abstract: This document exercises every supported feature of Calepin in a single file.
keywords:
  - test
  - features
bibliography: library.bib
number-sections: true
sample_size: 50
features:
  - R code chunks
  - Python code chunks
  - Cross-references
show_extra: true
---

# Markdown basics {#sec-basics}

This section tests standard CommonMark and GFM features.

## Inline formatting

*Italic*, **bold**, ***bold italic***, ~~strikethrough~~, and `inline code`.

## Lists

Ordered:

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

Unordered:

- Alpha
- Beta
- Gamma

## Blockquote

> This is a blockquote with **bold** and *italic* text.

## Links and images

A [link to nowhere](https://example.com "Example") and a reference-style [link][ref1].

[ref1]: https://example.com

![Placeholder image](https://placehold.co/200x100)

## Footnotes

Here is a sentence with a footnote[^fn1].

[^fn1]: This is the footnote content.

## Horizontal rule

---

## Table {#sec-tables}

| Name  | Score | Grade |
|-------|------:|:-----:|
| Alice |    95 | A     |
| Bob   |    87 | B     |
| Carol |    92 | A     |


# Code chunks

## R chunks

```{r}
x <- 1:10
mean(x)
```

### Echo fenced

```{r}
#| echo: fenced
summary(mtcars$mpg)
```

### Figure from R {#sec-rfig}

```{r, fig-scatter}
#| fig-cap: "Scatter plot of x vs x squared"
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| out-width: "80%"
plot(1:10, (1:10)^2, pch = 19, col = "steelblue",
     xlab = "x", ylab = "x squared")
```

See @fig-scatter for the scatter plot.

### Echo false

```{r}
#| echo: false
invisible("this code is hidden")
```

### Eval false

```{r}
#| eval: false
stop("this would error if evaluated")
```

### Include false (silent execution)

```{r}
#| include: false
secret_value <- 42
```

### Results asis

```{r}
#| results: asis
cat("**This is bold from asis output.**\n")
```

### Warning and message control

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
warning("suppressed warning")
message("suppressed message")
cat("Only this output appears.\n")
```

## Python chunks

```{python}
import math
print(f"Pi is approximately {math.pi:.4f}")
```

```{python}
squares = [x**2 for x in range(1, 6)]
print(squares)
```

## Shell chunks

```{sh}
echo "Hello from shell"
```

## Inline code

The mean of 1 to 10 is `{r} mean(1:10)`. Pi is `{python} f"{__import__('math').pi:.2f}"`.
The secret value is `{r} secret_value`.


# Math {#sec-math}

## Inline math

The Pythagorean theorem states $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$.

A literal dollar sign costs \$5.

## Display math

$$
\int_0^1 x^2 \, dx = \frac{1}{3}
$$

## Labeled equation

$$
E = mc^2
$$ {#eq-einstein}

## Multi-line equation

$$
\begin{aligned}
(a + b)^2 &= a^2 + 2ab + b^2 \\
(a - b)^2 &= a^2 - 2ab + b^2
\end{aligned}
$$ {#eq-binomial}

See @eq-einstein and @eq-binomial.


# Cross-references {#sec-crossref}

We can reference sections (@sec-basics, @sec-math), figures (@fig-scatter), tables (@tbl-planets), equations (@eq-einstein), theorems (@thm-fermat), definitions (@def-group), callouts (@nte-important), and code listings (@lst-example).

Bracketed form: [@fig-scatter]. Suppress type: [-@eq-einstein]. Grouped: [@eq-einstein; @eq-binomial].


# Citations

Narrative citation: @arel2026quantitative. Parenthetical: [@arel2026quantitative]. Year only: [-@arel2026quantitative].


# Callouts

::: {.callout-note #nte-important}
## A note
This is a **note** callout with a custom title and an ID for cross-referencing.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
A tip callout with the default title.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Watch out
A warning callout.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
An important callout with the default title.
:::

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## Collapsible caution
This caution callout starts collapsed.
:::


# Theorems {#sec-theorems}

::: {.theorem #thm-fermat}
## Fermat's Last Theorem
For $n > 2$, there are no positive integers $a$, $b$, $c$ such that $a^n + b^n = c^n$.
:::

::: {.proof}
The proof was completed by Andrew Wiles in 1995 and is too long to include here.
:::

::: {.definition #def-group}
## Group
A group is a set $G$ with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
:::

::: {.lemma #lem-helper}
Every finite group has an element of order dividing the group order.
:::

::: {.corollary #cor-consequence}
Every group of prime order is cyclic.
:::

::: {.example #exm-integers}
The integers under addition form a group.
:::

::: {.remark #rem-note}
Groups are fundamental to abstract algebra.
:::

By @thm-fermat and @def-group, we see the connection. Also see @lem-helper, @cor-consequence, @exm-integers, and @rem-note.


# Tabsets

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Tab A

Content of the first tab.

## Tab B

Content of the second tab with some math: $e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0$.

## Tab C

```{r}
cat("Code in a tab\n")
```

:::


# Layouts

## Two-column layout

::: {layout-ncol=2}

Left column content. This paragraph appears on the left side of a two-column layout.

Right column content. This paragraph appears on the right side.

:::

## Custom grid layout

::: {layout="[[1, 1, 1]]"}

![A](https://placehold.co/150x100)

![B](https://placehold.co/150x100)

![C](https://placehold.co/150x100)

:::


# Figure divs

::: {#fig-div-example}

![](https://placehold.co/300x200)

A figure div with a caption.
:::

See @fig-div-example.


# Table divs

::: {#tbl-planets}
| Planet  | Moons |
|---------|------:|
| Earth   |     1 |
| Mars    |     2 |
| Jupiter |    95 |

Number of known moons for selected planets.
:::

See @tbl-planets.


# Code listings

```{r, lst-example}
#| lst-cap: "A simple R function"
square <- function(x) x^2
square(7)
```

See @lst-example.


# Content visibility

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
This paragraph is only visible in **HTML** output.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="latex"}
This paragraph is only visible in **LaTeX** output.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="typst"}
This paragraph is only visible in **Typst** output.
:::

::: {.content-hidden when-format="html"}
This paragraph is hidden in HTML output but visible elsewhere.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-meta="show_extra"}
This paragraph is visible because `show_extra` is true.
:::

::: {.content-hidden when-meta="show_extra"}
This paragraph is hidden because `show_extra` is true.
:::


# Hidden div

::: {.hidden}
```{r}
hidden_result <- "computed but not shown"
```
:::

The hidden div executed: `{r} hidden_result`.


# Raw blocks

```{=html}
<div style="color: green; font-weight: bold;">This is raw HTML.</div>
```

```{=latex}
\textbf{This is raw \LaTeX{}.}
```

```{=typst}
#strong[This is raw Typst.]
```

```{=markdown}
**This is raw Markdown.**
```


# Jinja features

## Context variables

The document title is: {{ meta.title }}.

The sample size is: {{ var.sample_size }}.

The current format is: {{ engine }}.

## Conditionals

{% if engine == "html" %}
Rendering to HTML.
{% else %}
Rendering to {{ engine }}.
{% endif %}

## Loops

Features of this document:

{% for item in var.features %}
- {{ item }}
{% endfor %}

## Built-in spans

[]{.lorem sentences=2}

[]{.pagebreak}

After the page break.


# Syntax highlighting

Non-executable code blocks with syntax highlighting:

```javascript
function greet(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}
```

```python
def factorial(n: int) -> int:
    return 1 if n <= 1 else n * factorial(n - 1)
```


# Diagrams

```{mermaid}
graph LR
    A[Start] --> B{Decision}
    B -->|Yes| C[Action]
    B -->|No| D[End]
```


# References
